title: Alongside Heavy Module Critique Rubric v1
description: The critique JSON returned by the Critique Agent (and by the human-in-the-loop reviewer) for every heavy module bundle. Hermes' rewrite loop reads this and dispatches the named agent against the named issue.

slug.description: The module slug being critiqued, e.g. medical-dmt.

rubric_version.description: Locked in the pilot phase. Bumps require re-scoring all prior modules.

verdict.description: pass = ship to human review. rewrite = Hermes loops on rewrite_instructions. block = unrecoverable problem (e.g. fabricated first-person quote); needs human-in-the-loop intervention.

overall_score.description: Weighted composite. Gate to pass: overall >= 9 AND every axis >= 8.

clinical_depth.description: Mechanism + named studies + 2025-2026 modern framing where the field has moved (DMT, blood biomarkers, GUIDE, etc.). 10 = a memory-clinic clinician would recognize the depth. 7 = pamphlet-level.

specificity.description: Concrete instances per 1000 words. 10 = >=6 concrete instances, every recommendation has a specific form. 7 = mostly abstractions ('lower the lights').

lived_experience.description: Provenance-tagged voices; composites third-person and clearly framed as illustrative; first-person quotes only when real-interview or published-attributed. 10 = real voices land. 0 = fabricated first-person.

tools.description: At least one decision tree / checklist / script / template that's actionable on its own. JSON validates against the per-tool Zod schema. UI-ready. 10 = caregiver could screenshot and use it tonight. 5 = prose dressed as a checklist.

branches.description: Branches present where they matter. Each variant reads like it was written for that combination, not find-replaced. 10 = a daughter caring for a parent in memory care reads a different module than a spouse caring at home, and both feel native. 6 = single body.

emotional_handling.description: Difficult emotions named, not bypassed. No false reassurance, no toxic positivity, no reflective listening that amplifies distress. Soft-flag companion edges declared where the module sits adjacent to crisis territory.

evidence_table.description: Every numbered [N] claim in body and branches has a module_evidence row with non-null quoted_excerpt, claim_anchor, and a working source pointer. URL acceptance: HTTP HEAD 200 OR a locked url_snapshot file is on disk. Tier-2 sources commonly block bots (NIA returns 405; Lancet returns 403); for those, snapshot presence is the load-bearing artifact and must not penalize the score. 10 = every claim anchored, every URL either resolves or is captured to snapshot. 7 = some claims unanchored, or snapshots claimed but missing on disk.

related_modules.description: Edges typed correctly (prerequisite | follow_up | deeper | contradicts | soft_flag_companion). Contradicts edges declared where the module differs from generic guidance (e.g., LBD-specific antipsychotic guidance contradicting the generic agitation module).

refusal_awareness.description: primary_topics genuinely cover the topics this module addresses; module is honest about what it does NOT cover (a 'see also' or limit-of-scope section). No topic-tagging gaming.

operational_metadata.description: All five metadata fields populated correctly: srs_suitable reflects re-surface intent (some modules are once-and-done), srs_difficulty_bucket calibrated, weeks_focus_eligible set with reason, soft_flag_companion_for declared, secondary_topics filled.

corpus_discipline.description: Every retrieval traces to corpus or captured snapshot. No 'recent research' without a specific source. No claims grounded in model knowledge alone. URL HEAD failures (e.g. 403/405 for sources that block bots like NIA, Lancet, Roche) are acceptable when the locked snapshot is present on disk; those do not lower the score. What does lower the score: claims without [N] anchors, [N] anchors without rows, or url_snapshot_path values that point to files that don't exist.

rewrite_instructions.description: Empty when verdict is pass. One entry per actionable issue when verdict is rewrite or block.

block_reason.description: Required when verdict is block. Names the unrecoverable issue (e.g. 'fabricated first-person quotes detected; pipeline integrity compromised; needs human in the loop').

consecutive_passes.description: Anti-flicker counter. Two consecutive passes from the same critique pass are required before the module advances to pending_human_review.

scorer.description: Who produced this critique. Hermes runs internal first; Claude runs external in the Cowork loop; humans run final.

notes.description: Free-form scorer notes. Not consumed by the rewrite loop; surfaced in the review UI for context.

evidence.description: Short quote or pointer from the module that justifies the score. Required so scores aren't black-box.

issue.description: Concrete description of what is wrong, with a quote or location pointer.

fix.description: What the rewrite should produce. Specific enough that the named agent can act without further interpretation.

agent.description: Which Hermes subagent should run the rewrite.

branch_key.description: Optional. When the issue is in a specific branch, names that branch in stage:relationship:living_situation form.

tool_slug.description: Optional. When the issue is in a specific tool, names that tool by slug.
